creator: thierry@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Fri 25 mai 07 at 5:59:23 +0200, Mark Linimon <linimon@FreeBSD.org>
 wrote:
> Synopsis: Mk/bsd.port.mk: make ldconfig permanent for non-default prefixes.
> 
> State-Changed-From-To: open->feedback
> State-Changed-By: linimon
> State-Changed-When: Fri May 25 03:58:41 UTC 2007
> State-Changed-Why: 
> How many ports are affected by this?  I think it could be argued that these
> are bugs in the individual ports.

Since PREFIX is set by a '?=' in Mk/bsd.port.mk, it is legitim to affect
non default values, and in this case the problem will arise in every
port using LD_CONFIG.

net/mpich2 modifies PREFIX inside the port, and I don't think that a lot
of ports do that, but the same problem affects any user installing a
port in a non-standard PREFIX (e.g. if you INSTALL_AS_USER under your
$HOME).

Regards,
-- 
Th. Thomas.
